mission.title: nats: Consumer filter subjects stop matching after leader step-down
mission.description: Consumer filter subjects stop matching after leader step-down. Requested by 12+ users.

steps.title: Check current nats deployment
steps.description:
Verify your nats version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n nats -l app.kubernetes.io/name=nats
helm list -n nats 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working nats installation.

steps.title: Review nats configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant nats configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n nats -l app.kubernetes.io/name=nats
kubectl get configmap -n nats -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=nats
```
### Observed behavior

Consumer stops working after filterSubject has been modified (edit) and the consumer leader has been stepped down.
Also all messages are dropped even if they match the filter subjects.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Consumer filter subjects stop matching after leader
steps.description:
An interest-based replicated stream could desync after a consumer filter update, with new messages being persisted on some but not all replicas. This was due to the sublist being cleared and only updated for the server that's the consumer leader. The stream on the consumer leader would persist the
```yaml
# other params are default
nats stream add foo-stream  --subjects "foo.>" --replicas 5 --storage file --retention interest --ack --discard old --dupe-window 2m --no-allow-direct

# other params are default
nats consumer add foo-stream foo-consumer --pull --filter "foo.bar" --filter "foo.bar1" --deliver all  --ack explicit    --replay instant   --max-deliver 20   --max-pending 1000
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n nats -l app.kubernetes.io/name=nats
kubectl get events -n nats --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Consumer filter subjects stop matching after leader" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: An interest-based replicated stream could desync after a consumer filter update, with new messages being persisted on some but not all replicas. This was due to the sublist being cleared and only updated for the server that's the consumer leader. The stream on the consumer leader would persist the messages, but followers wouldn't.
codeSnippets:
# other params are default
nats stream add foo-stream  --subjects "foo.>" --replicas 5 --storage file --retention interest --ack --discard old --dupe-window 2m --no-allow-direct

# other params are default
nats consumer add foo-stream foo-consumer --pull --filter "foo.bar" --filter "foo.bar1" --deliver all  --ack explicit    --replay instant   --max-deliver 20   --max-pending 1000

# use 2nd terminal to put some data in
nats pub "foo.bar" 'check {{ .Count }}' --sleep 1s --count 5

# use 3rd terminal to get messages from the consumer
nats consumer next foo-stream foo-consumer --wait 2s --count 5

# use 4th terminal to monitor the consumer state itself
watch nats consumer info foo-stream foo-consumer

nats consumer edit foo-stream foo-consumer  --filter "foo.bar" --filter "foo.bar2"

# ==> right here Step 2 has expected behavior

nats consumer cluster step-down foo-stream foo-consumer

# ==> staring here Step 2 has unexpected behavior

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with nats installed or the issue environment reproducible.
